steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank fills in the noun phrase inside "The gaseous ______ has a molar mass of 162.15 g/mol." The name "quindoxin" identifies which gaseous compound is meant. The question is whether that name is essential (restrictive) or optional (nonrestrictive), and how commas should reflect that.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'compound, quindoxin (C₈H₆N₂O₂),': Commas on both sides mark a nonrestrictive appositive — meaning the name is optional extra info. But without the name, "the gaseous compound" is vague and doesn't identify which compound. The name is essential, so commas are wrong. ✗
  • Choice B — 'compound quindoxin (C₈H₆N₂O₂)': No commas. "Quindoxin" sits directly beside "compound" as a restrictive appositive that identifies which gaseous compound is meant. No commas correctly signals this essential relationship. ✓
  • Choice C — 'compound quindoxin (C₈H₆N₂O₂),': A single trailing comma incorrectly places a boundary between the subject noun phrase and its verb "has." Nothing after the comma warrants this pause, and the comma is unmatched. ✗
  • Choice D — 'compound, quindoxin (C₈H₆N₂O₂)': An opening comma without a closing comma creates an unmatched, malformed appositive. Even if nonrestrictive were intended, both commas are required. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
